audit: invAIriant self-audit — the framework as its own first subject
claim: Nothing cross-checks that a config's mandatory_lenses name lenses that actually exist; a typo passes validation but has no lens file, so the audit silently cannot run that lens.

audit: invAIriant self-audit — the framework as its own first subject
claim: The example report JSON references findings CNV-043 and CNV-044 in its lens verdicts and executive summary but never defines them in findings[]; only CNV-041/042 exist there, while the prose example defines CNV-041..044.

audit: invAIriant self-audit #2 — the CLI, skill, and audit-memory build (phases 1–6)
claim: Audit memory is resolved relative to the current working directory, so running the CLI from a subdirectory silently misses the committed .invairiant/history/.

audit: invAIriant self-audit #2 — the CLI, skill, and audit-memory build (phases 1–6)
claim: The `collect` grep fallback (no ripgrep) iterates every tracked file until the per-category cap is hit, so on a very large repo with few matches it scans the whole tree.

audit: invAIriant self-audit #2 — the CLI, skill, and audit-memory build (phases 1–6)
claim: The CLI is covered by CI smokes only; there is no unit-test suite for the semantic linter, record idempotency, or render logic.
